sections.paragraph:
Ἰταλία, ας, ἡ (Soph., Ant. 1119; Hdt. 1, 24 al.; SIG 1229, 2 πλεύσας εἰς Ἰταλίαν; Philo; SibOr; oft. Joseph.) always w. the art. (B-D-F §261, 6) Italy Ac 18:2 (Jos., Ant. 16, 7 ἧκον ἀπὸ τῆς ʼΙτ.); 27:1, 6; Hb subscr. (no art.). οἱ ἀπὸ τ. Ἰταλίας Hb 13:24; s. ἀπό 3b.—Kl.-Pauly II, 1479–85 (lit.).

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

art. art. = article

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ιτ.̠It. = Itala, early version of the NT in Latin

subscr. subscr. = subscription

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 484
